title: Listening - Part 4: Restoring Unwanted Furniture
description: You will hear part of a radio interview with a man called Ravi, who restores unwanted furniture. For questions 24–30, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).

content.audio_script: Presenter: My final guest today is Ravi Patel, who gives unwanted furniture a new life in his workshop. Ravi, were you trained as a carpenter? || Ravi: Not at first. I worked in administration and did practical projects only at weekends. The turning point was personal. [24]My aunt gave me an old wooden chair with a broken leg, and repairing it made me realise I preferred restoring useful objects to buying replacements[/24]. I began taking evening classes, then reduced my office hours until the workshop could support me. || Presenter: Where do you find the furniture you restore? || Ravi: Some customers bring family pieces, but I also need inexpensive items for sale. I never simply take furniture left outside, because it may still belong to someone. [25]Local builders contact me when houses are being renovated, and with the owner's permission I collect pieces that would otherwise be thrown away[/25]. It’s surprising what people decide they no longer want. || Presenter: Do customers want old furniture to look completely new? || Ravi: Sometimes, but not usually. A table with a history shouldn’t necessarily look as if it left a factory yesterday. [26]Many customers ask me to keep small marks in the wood because they show the object's previous life[/26]. I repair damage and make furniture safe, but I don’t erase every sign of age. || Presenter: You also teach weekend courses. What do beginners struggle with? || Ravi: People are enthusiastic and want to start cutting wood immediately. That creates mistakes. [27]The lesson they resist most, but need most, is measuring patiently before they use any tool[/27]. If the basic measurements are wrong, even excellent decorating cannot make a drawer fit properly. || Presenter: Does using old wood create particular problems? || Ravi: Yes. You don’t always know what happened to it in the past. [28]The biggest risk is finding hidden nails or screws while cutting, which can damage tools and be dangerous[/28]. I inspect every piece carefully before work begins, though occasionally something still surprises me. || Presenter: You share restoration videos online. Are they useful for business? || Ravi: Very. At first I posted perfect before-and-after photos, but they didn’t explain the amount of skill involved. [29]People became much more interested when I showed the slow process, including difficulties and decisions along the way[/29]. Viewers seem to enjoy understanding why a repair works, not just seeing a shiny result. || Presenter: Do you have plans for the future? || Ravi: I don’t want a larger shop full of machines. [30]I’d rather offer apprenticeships so younger people can learn repair skills and see that working with their hands can provide a satisfying career[/30]. There is already enough furniture in the world; we need more people able to care for it. || Presenter: Ravi, thank you. || Ravi: Thank you.

task.title: Part 4
task.instruction: For questions 24–30, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).

questions.question: What made Ravi begin thinking about furniture restoration as a career?

options.A: He completed a woodworking course through his office.
options.B: He repaired a chair that had been given to him.
options.C: He wanted to replace furniture in his own home.

questions.explanation: Repairing an old chair from his aunt made Ravi realise he preferred restoration to replacement.

questions.question: How does Ravi obtain furniture to restore?

options.A: He collects unwanted furniture with the owners' agreement.
options.B: He buys old items directly from building companies.
options.C: He takes furniture that has been placed outside homes.

questions.explanation: Builders let him collect unwanted pieces during renovations once owners give permission.

questions.question: What do many of Ravi's customers prefer?

options.A: Furniture which has been painted in a modern style.
options.B: Furniture which looks completely new again.
options.C: Furniture which still shows something of its age.

questions.explanation: Customers often ask him to retain small marks that reveal an object's past.

questions.question: What important point does Ravi teach beginners?

options.A: They must take time to measure correctly.
options.B: They should begin with decorative techniques.
options.C: They need to use professional tools immediately.

questions.explanation: He stresses patient measuring before students start using tools.

questions.question: What is a danger of working with old wood?

options.A: It may be unsuitable for making furniture safe.
options.B: It may contain metal pieces that are not visible.
options.C: It may lose its colour during cutting.

questions.explanation: Hidden nails or screws can damage equipment and create danger.

questions.question: Why are Ravi's online videos now more successful?

options.A: They show the work and decisions involved in restoration.
options.B: They allow customers to choose new designs quickly.
options.C: They compare the prices of old and new furniture.

questions.explanation: Viewers became more interested when he showed the restoration process and its challenges.

questions.question: What does Ravi hope to do in the future?

options.A: Buy machinery so he can restore more furniture.
options.B: Open another workshop in a nearby town.
options.C: Train young people in practical repair skills.

questions.explanation: He would prefer to offer apprenticeships and pass on repair skills.
